description: According to the Council on Foreign Relations, the comparison relies on incomplete data and fails to capture the sharp rise in China's surplus since late 2024. During the same period, the eurozone's surplus fell from around 420 billion euros to 280 billion euros.

First ‘cell rejuvenation’ therapy in humans: Can ageing finally be treated?
description: The innovative cell rejuvenation therapy, known as ER-100, is set to enter human trials, focused on enhancing the functionality of aging cells rather than attempting to reverse the aging process. This revolutionary approach shows promise in potentially decelerating age-related decline and addressing conditions like Alzheimer's. However, medical professionals advise caution due to ongoing research into long-term safety and efficacy, indicating a shift in understanding aging as a fixed outcome.

How proposed US citizenship fee hike will impact applicants; Indians among hardest hit
description: US Citizenship and Immigration Services plans a significant hike in naturalisation application fees, potentially costing Indian immigrants much more to become American citizens.  The proposed increase for the primary application form could reach $1,330, with reduced-fee options and waivers being eliminated.  This comes as Indians remain a major group seeking US citizenship.

Message seen on WhatsApp: Kunal Shah gets real CRED from Meta
description: Kunal Shah is an interesting choice, because WhatsApp does not need a leader to teach people how to send a message; it needs one who understands how trust, status, habit, and small transactions mutate inside closed networks, which is, almost exactly, the discipline CRED was built to practise.

title: Keir Starmer quits: Is post-Brexit Britain too difficult to govern?
Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
description: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

Diabetes cure finally found? First drug treating type 1 diabetes made available
description: In a landmark announcement, the NHS has introduced a beacon of hope for individuals susceptible to type 1 diabetes. Teplizumab, an innovative medication, is now able to delay the disease's onset for an average of three years, focusing on the autoimmune process impacting the pancreas. This breakthrough provides vital time for young patients, allowing them to manage their health more effectively before the disease fully manifests.

title: Henry David Thoreau’s quote on self-belief: A timeless lesson in shaping destiny
description: Henry David Thoreau, a 19th-century philosopher and naturalist, championed self-reliance and living by one's own values. His time at Walden Pond inspired his seminal work, urging readers to simplify life and connect with nature. Thoreau's belief that self-perception shapes destiny, as highlighted in his quote, remains profoundly relevant, reminding us that inner attitudes often dictate our path more than external circumstances.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
description: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: When breath turned pungent mid-sleep: Ammonia leak survivors recount ordeal
description: A devastating ammonia leak at a seafood processing factory in Tiruvallur district left workers, many young women far from home, in critical condition. The gas, originating from the processing area, seeped into sleeping quarters, incapacitating those off-duty. Emergency services faced delays, with many suffering severe respiratory distress and other ailments. Volunteers and authorities responded, but the incident highlights the vulnerability of workers in such facilities.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots
description: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
description: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.
